headline: General Mills agrees to sell Häagen-Dazs shops in China to investor group
summary: General Mills is selling its Häagen-Dazs ice-cream shops in mainland China to an investor group that includes Chinese tea brand Ningji.  Minneapolis-based General Mills said in a statement late Monday that the deal will allow the buyers to exclusively sell the Häagen-Dazs brand in ice cream shops and gifting businesses across mainland China.  General Mills will continue to sell Häagen-Dazs ice cream to Chinese retail and food service operations.

headline: General Mills offloads Häagen-Dazs shops in China
summary: General Mills said the sale “elevates the company’s focus on its brands and channels that provide the strongest opportunities for profitable growth”.

headline: UBS Maintains Sell on General Mills, Lowers Price Target to $30
summary: UBS  analyst Peter Grom   maintains General Mills (NYSE:GIS) with a Sell and lowers the price target from $35 to $30.
